title: The Mystery of the Missing Kelp Forest
summary: Deep at the bottom of the ocean, a mermaid family—Mamma Blue, Dadda Yellow, and their daughter Pink—lives in a house with a beautiful backyard kelp forest. During a playdate with their new friend Physi-Ona the Rainbow Murgirl, they discover their entire kelp forest has vanished. Following a trail of giant circular footprints and chewed-up kelp, they consult their neighbor Crabby, who leads them to a nearby sea cave. There, they find a family of bears who had unknowingly eaten the garden. To make things right, the bears and the murgirls join forces to plant a new, even larger kelp forest for the otters to enjoy.

highlightQuote.text: No, I'm new to this world. I'm new to underwater because I'm only one year old... No, I'm only six years old.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: All right, it's time for bed.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Can we repeat the stars and stories with you?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Can you what?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Can I repeat the stars and stories, says the murgirl.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What do you mean repeat the stars and stories?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Repeat everything you said.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, well we could try that. It's all set up if you want to turn out the light or should I?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I should. You hold the lantern.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, hello murgirl. What's your name again?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Finley Fawn.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Finley Fawn? Okay Finley Fawn, look up! Wow, stars! We never see those from the bottom of the ocean. They're beautiful.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: This lantern is making them. Oh, they're moving!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I see. Because the lantern's tipping. All right, who should we tell a stars and stories story about?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Let's tell a story about Pink Murgirl and Yellow Murgirl.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, do you want to start it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes. But first you've got to meet them. This is Yellow Murgirl, Blue Rainbow Murgirl... Rainbow Murgirl, this is Yellow Murgirl.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, nice to meet you. I'll hold him under the light.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hello, I am Yellow Murgirl. Pink bar best, in our best, in our best...

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, what was that song? I liked it.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And we are Pink Murgirl and Blue Murgirl.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh hello, Yellow, Pink and Blue Murgirls. So we're going to tell a story about all of them.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes, Blue Murgirl, Yellow Murgirl and Pink Murgirl.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, how does it begin Isabella?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It begins, the murgirls were in their house.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: The murgirls were in their house, yes. And what happened?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, you've got to be telling the story.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well once upon a time the murgirls were in their house. The Pink, the Yellow and the Blue Murgirl were all in their house.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And this one's in my lap, please.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yes, you can sit in my lap and Rainbow Murgirl can sit in your lap. And then all of a sudden they heard something unexpected. What was the sound that they heard?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They heard their doorbell.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And what did their doorbell sound like?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Their doorbell sounded like... did it sound like blub blub? Yeah, it sounded like blub blub.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, that's our underwater doorbell, said Pink Murgirl. I wonder who it is? And Yellow Murgirl says, well I wasn't expecting anyone, were you? And Blue Murgirl said no. And Blue Murgirl went and opened the door, and what did she see?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She saw Rainbow Murgirl!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, well, hello... who is it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's Physi-Ona Murgirl, the Rainbow Murgirl.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well nice to meet you. What can I do for you?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I just want to have a playdate with you.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A playdate with us? That's a nice idea. What kind of things do you like to play with?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I like to play with stuff like it... stuff like nibbling on kelp.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Nibbling on kelp? Well right in our backyard we have a small kelp forest of our own. Would you like to come and see it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yep.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And so Blue Murgirl introduced the Rainbow Murgirl to her sisters Pink and Yellow and they all went...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, Blue and Yellow are Mamma and Dadda and then the Pink Murgirl is the kiddo. So she shows Rainbow Murgirl.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Ah okay. And so Pink, who's the kiddo murgirl, shows Rainbow Murgirl around the back of the house. And before they can all get into the backyard, Pink Murgirl says, Oh my goodness, what happened?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: What happened with what?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: There was a big problem. A big surprise. Something unexpected in their backyard. Or something that, more precisely you could say, was not in the backyard.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Um... so Dad, what was it that you said? It should be something that's not there.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, the kelp forest was gone! And the Pink Murgirl said, Oh no!

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Go and ask your parents, okay? What happened to the kelp forest? We spent so long trying to grow it and it was so beautiful and the otters liked it so much. Mom, Dad, what happened?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And so Blue and Yellow Murgirls came outside to look in the yard and said, Oh dear, I see there's a problem here, isn't there? And Pink Murgirl said, What do you think happened? Who might have done this? Do you know, Rainbow Murgirl? And what did Rainbow Murgirl say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, I'm new to this world. I'm new to underwater because I'm only one year old... No, I'm only six years old.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I see. What kinds of things do you like to eat, Rainbow Murgirl?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Um, I like to eat kelp.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, you do. You do like to eat kelp, I see. And could you just quickly open your mouth for me please so I can look at your teeth?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Ahhh.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And did Pink Murgirl see anything stuck between Rainbow Murgirl's teeth, like maybe little pieces of kelp or anything?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, she saw nothing.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Nothing. Oh, okay. Thanks Rainbow Murgirl, just had to check. Maybe we should look closely at the little stubs of all the kelp on the sea floor to see if there's any clues that whoever did this might have left behind.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They saw lots of teeny tiny pieces of kelp.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, tiny pieces of kelp. Hmm. Whoever did this must have chewed up the kelp really well. Were there any other clues?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: There were footprints!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, and what shape were the footprints?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They were shaped really big. They were giant. Giant footprints, but they just went in circles until they led to a place where a kelp forest used to be. And then there was some... footprints. So it was a mama bear and a baby bear.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, a giant mama bear and a giant baby bear?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, a small baby bear and a giant mama bear. And that's who ate the kelp, you think? Yeah. But I don't think so, actually... let me...

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Blue and Yellow Murgirls said, My goodness, that is a very interesting clue. We'd better go and ask our friend who knows about the local bears if they know anything about it. It was their friend next door named...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Crabby.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Crabby! Yeah... The kelp forest is gone in our yard! Oh, I'm so sorry to hear that. I loved that kelp forest. What happened to it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: We don't know, but there were some big and small footprints that led in circles until they led to the place where the kelp forest used to be.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well that seems like an important clue. And whose footprints were they?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I'll come and have a look. And Crabby scuttled over to their backyard and said, You know, I'm pretty sure these are bear footprints. And there's only one bear family that lives around here.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Or there must have been some moving in who wanted to eat it.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Right, they're a new family. They just moved in. Maybe they didn't know the kelp forest belonged to you. Let's go ask 'em.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Wait, wait, wait, wait. Wait for what? Wait for this. There's one more clue. There's some tiny pieces of kelp dropped over the yard, the murgirl yard.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, sometimes when bears eat kelp, they have to chew it a lot, and so it turns into lots of tiny pieces, so that might be why. And Crabby led everyone to a little sea cave not far away, and it was surrounded all around by more of the same bear footprints. And inside they heard a funny sound like this: zzzzz...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Wake up, wake up, says Rainbow Murgirl.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, who is it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's Rainbow Murgirl. Did you eat the kelp forest in Pink Murgirl, Yellow Murgirl and Blue Murgirl's backyard?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, we did eat a big kelp forest earlier today. It was delicious. We were so hungry, but we didn't know it belonged to anyone. Was that your kelp forest?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yep, it was ours.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I'm so sorry.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And the otters loved it.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: The otters, oh I love otters! Well how can we help? What can we do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: You can help by seeing some footprints... there were tiny bear footprints from the baby bear, I guess, and there was giant footprints.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, that was me and the baby bear, those were my baby's footprints, he liked eating the kelp too. But can we do something to help you out, like maybe help you get a new forest planted or plant something else?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Let's get a new forest planted.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Because this is Rainbow Murgirl.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, hi Rainbow Murgirl. Can you help us plant the new kelp forest?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes. And let's plant it bigger and even better this time, and we will promise not to eat it by accident.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And so they all worked together to plant a new kelp forest. And that's the end of the story.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'm the first one to knock over the lantern!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay. There we go.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I knocked over the lantern.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: You sure did.

characters.name: Finley Fawn
characters.description: The mermaid persona adopted by Izzy at the beginning of the story.

characters.name: Pink Murgirl
characters.description: The 'kiddo' mermaid who loves her backyard kelp forest.

characters.name: Yellow Murgirl
characters.description: The 'dadda' mermaid and part of the murgirl family.

characters.name: Blue Murgirl
characters.description: The 'mamma' mermaid who opens the door for the visitor.

characters.name: Rainbow Mergirl
characters.description: A resourceful mergirl known for her clever use of kelp to aid the Lanternshark, she balances her practical ingenuity with a spirited streak, recently exemplified by a sharp disagreement with the Pink Mergirl over their cherished stuffed animals.

characters.name: Crabby
characters.description: A neighbor who helps identify the mysterious footprints in the yard.

characters.name: Mama Bear
characters.description: The colossal mother bear discovered at the first story’s conclusion, a well-meaning giant whose accidental grazing once laid waste to the murgirls' vibrant kelp forest.

characters.name: Baby Bear
characters.description: The small bear who accompanies the mama bear in eating the kelp.

places.name: Bottom of the ocean
places.description: The general underwater setting where the murgirls live.

places.name: The Murgirl House
places.description: The home where Blue, Yellow, and Pink Murgirl live.

places.name: The Kelp Forest
places.description: The backyard garden where the murgirls grow kelp for otters.

places.name: Crabby's House
places.description: The residence of the murgirls' knowledgeable neighbor.

places.name: The Sea Cave
places.description: The resting place of the bear family who ate the kelp.
